content.text: A production model is usually deployed behind an API gateway that performs {{c1::routing}}, {{c2::rate limiting}}, and {{c3::request logging}}.
content.explanation: Deployment is not only about serving a model; it also requires secure traffic control, quotas, and observability.
content.spanish: Un modelo de producción suele desplegarse detrás de una pasarela API que realiza enrutamiento, limitación de velocidad y registro de solicitudes.
content.usage: Core pattern for enterprise AI systems and internal LLM applications.

content.text: Observability in ML systems means tracking {{c1::latency}}, {{c2::cost}}, and {{c3::drift}} across model versions and user segments.
content.explanation: A model can work technically and still fail in production if teams cannot see quality degradation or rising costs.
content.spanish: La observabilidad en sistemas de ML significa rastrear latencia, costo y deriva entre versiones de modelos y segmentos de usuarios.
content.usage: Essential for model monitoring dashboards and incident response.

content.text: Data drift occurs when the distribution of incoming features changes, while concept drift happens when the target relationship itself {{c1::changes}}.
content.explanation: Drift detection is critical because a model trained on old patterns may become unreliable in a new context.
content.spanish: La deriva de datos ocurre cuando cambia la distribución de las características, mientras que la deriva conceptual ocurre cuando cambia la relación con el objetivo.
content.usage: Used in monitoring pipelines for finance, fraud, and recommendation systems.

content.text: Prompt caching stores reusable prefixes so the system can avoid recomputing the same {{c1::context}} for repeated requests.
content.explanation: This reduces latency and token cost for large-system prompts and multi-turn conversations.
content.spanish: El almacenamiento en caché de prompts guarda prefijos reutilizables para evitar recomputar el mismo contexto en solicitudes repetidas.
content.usage: Very useful for assistants, copilots, and enterprise knowledge bots.

content.text: A robust deployment pipeline includes a {{c1::rollback strategy}} so teams can revert to a previous model version quickly when issues appear.
content.explanation: Rollback is a crucial operational safeguard for AI systems because failures are often discovered only after release.
content.spanish: Un pipeline de despliegue robusto incluye una estrategia de reversión para restaurar rápidamente una versión anterior del modelo si aparecen problemas.
content.usage: Common in continuous delivery pipelines and model registries.

content.text: A canary deployment routes {{c1::a small percentage (e.g., 1-5%) of production traffic}} to the new model version before rolling it out fully.
content.explanation: Named after canary birds in coal mines. If the new model version has problems, only a fraction of users are affected. Monitoring during canary phase includes: latency, error rate, output quality metrics (if applicable), and downstream business KPIs. Canary percentage is gradually increased as confidence grows. Common tools: Kubernetes Argo Rollouts, AWS CodeDeploy, Google Cloud Run traffic splitting.
content.spanish: Un despliegue canary enruta un pequeño porcentaje (ej. 1-5%) del tráfico de producción a la nueva versión del modelo antes de desplegarlo por completo. Si hay problemas, solo una fracción de usuarios se ve afectada.
content.usage:
• "We're running a 5% canary on the new recommendation model. If p95 latency stays under 200ms and CTR holds steady over 24 hours, we'll ramp to 50%."
  • Monitoring checklist during canary: (1) error rate delta, (2) latency percentiles, (3) business metric (conversion, CTR), (4) fairness metric if applicable.

content.text: In a blue-green deployment, {{c1::two identical production environments (blue = current, green = new) run simultaneously}}, with instant traffic switch and zero-downtime rollback.
content.explanation: Blue-green is safer than canary for models that can't serve mixed versions simultaneously (e.g., models with shared state or session context). The tradeoff is cost: running two full environments doubles infrastructure spend. Rollback is instant — just switch traffic back to blue. Key consideration for ML: ensure feature engineering pipelines are also versioned.
content.spanish: En un despliegue blue-green, dos entornos de producción idénticos (blue = actual, green = nuevo) corren simultáneamente, con cambio de tráfico instantáneo y rollback sin tiempo de inactividad. Más seguro que canary para modelos con estado compartido.
content.usage:
• "We maintain blue-green for our fraud detection model because mixing v1 and v2 predictions on the same transaction would create inconsistency. Green was validated for 48h in shadow mode before the switch."
  • Rollback command: re-point load balancer from green to blue — typically < 30 seconds.

content.text: Shadow deployment means {{c1::the new model receives the same production traffic as the old one, generates predictions, but those predictions are NOT served to users — only logged for comparison}}.
content.explanation: Shadow mode is the safest pre-production validation strategy. It allows you to compare new model outputs with old model outputs on real traffic, without any risk to users. Critically, it catches distribution mismatch between training and production data that offline evaluation misses. The shadow model is essentially free-riding on prod traffic.
content.spanish: El despliegue en modo sombra significa que el nuevo modelo recibe el mismo tráfico de producción, genera predicciones, pero esas predicciones NO se sirven a los usuarios — solo se registran para comparación. La estrategia de validación pre-producción más segura.
content.usage:
• "We ran the new NER model in shadow mode for 2 weeks. Compared to production model outputs, 94.3% agreement on entity detection with notable improvements on Spanish-language tickets."
  • Shadow mode requires: (1) duplicate inference pipeline, (2) A/B logging infrastructure, (3) comparison metrics defined in advance.

content.text: A model registry is {{c1::a centralized store that tracks model versions, their training metadata, evaluation metrics, and deployment status — linking artifacts to experiments}}.
content.explanation: Without a model registry, it's impossible to answer 'which model is in production and what data was it trained on?' Tools: MLflow Model Registry, AWS SageMaker Model Registry, Google Vertex AI Model Registry, Weights & Biases. Each registered model should include: training dataset version, hyperparameters, evaluation metrics, and a link to the training run. This is the 'source of truth' for model lifecycle management.
content.spanish: Un registro de modelos es un almacén centralizado que rastrea versiones del modelo, sus metadatos de entrenamiento, métricas de evaluación y estado de despliegue — vinculando artefactos a experimentos. El 'source of truth' para la gestión del ciclo de vida del modelo.
content.usage:
• Model registry record example: {model_name: 'fraud-detector-v2.3', stage: 'Production', training_data: 'transactions_2025-Q4', accuracy: 0.934, F1: 0.921, registered_by: 'ML-team', deployed_to: 'fraud-api-prod'}
  • "Before deploying, always check the registry: is this the correct version? Is it marked as 'Production Ready'? What were its evaluation metrics?"

content.scenario: MLOps 📊: Data Drift vs. Concept Drift
content.text: Data drift is {{c1::a change in the input data distribution P(X)}}, while concept drift is {{c1::a change in the relationship between inputs and outputs P(Y|X)}}.
content.explanation: Data drift: the world changes (e.g., users start submitting tickets in a new language). The model still works correctly per its learned rules, but is now operating on a different distribution than it was trained on. Concept drift: the relationship itself changes (e.g., what was once a fraudulent transaction pattern is now normal behavior). Concept drift requires retraining; data drift may only require reweighting or feature engineering updates.
content.spanish: La deriva de datos es un cambio en la distribución de datos de entrada P(X). La deriva de concepto es un cambio en la relación entre entradas y salidas P(Y|X). La deriva de concepto requiere reentrenamiento; la de datos puede requerir solo re-ponderación.
content.usage:
• Data drift example: Your sentiment model was trained on formal product reviews; now users submit casual social media posts. Same task, different distribution.
  • Concept drift example: A fraud model trained pre-COVID labels certain transaction patterns as suspicious; post-COVID those patterns (large grocery orders, contactless payments) are normal.

content.text: PSI (Population Stability Index) measures {{c1::how much the distribution of a feature has shifted between training and serving}} — PSI < 0.1 is stable, 0.1-0.2 is minor shift, > 0.2 requires investigation.
content.explanation: PSI is one of the most widely used drift detection statistics in production ML systems (especially in finance/credit). It compares the distribution of a feature in training data vs. a recent production window. PSI is computed as: Σ (Actual% - Expected%) * ln(Actual%/Expected%). Values: <0.1 stable, 0.1-0.2 monitor, >0.2 retrain or investigate.
content.spanish: El PSI mide cuánto ha cambiado la distribución de una característica entre entrenamiento y producción. PSI < 0.1 es estable, 0.1-0.2 es cambio menor, > 0.2 requiere investigación. Ampliamente usado en sistemas ML en producción.
content.usage:
• "Our weekly PSI report flagged 'customer_age' feature with PSI=0.24. We investigated and found we'd onboarded a new SMB segment that skews younger. Model performance on that cohort was 8% worse."
  • Monitor PSI on: all input features, model output scores, and key business segments separately.

content.scenario: MLOps 📊: Monitoring Hierarchy for ML Systems
content.text: ML monitoring should cover 4 layers: {{c1::(1) Infrastructure (CPU/memory/latency), (2) Input data quality, (3) Model performance metrics, (4) Business KPIs}}, in that order of detection speed.
content.explanation: Infrastructure alerts fire first (seconds). Input data quality issues show up in minutes-to-hours. Model degradation can take days to become statistically significant. Business KPI impact may take weeks. This hierarchy means you need monitoring at all 4 layers because waiting for business impact before detecting model issues is too late. Most teams monitor only layer 1; elite MLOps teams monitor all 4.
content.spanish: El monitoreo ML debe cubrir 4 capas: (1) Infraestructura, (2) Calidad de datos de entrada, (3) Métricas de rendimiento del modelo, (4) KPIs de negocio. La mayoría de los equipos solo monitorea la capa 1; los equipos élite de MLOps monitorean las 4.
content.usage:
• Layer 1: p95 latency > 500ms → PagerDuty alert
  • Layer 2: Null rate in 'customer_tier' feature > 5% → data quality alert
  • Layer 3: Model accuracy on weekly holdout < baseline - 3σ → model health alert
  • Layer 4: Conversion rate drops 8% week-over-week → business KPI alert (correlation with model change?)

content.text: LLM production systems require monitoring {{c1::output quality (faithfulness, relevance, toxicity)}}, {{c1::latency and token usage}}, and {{c1::retrieval quality}} (for RAG systems).
content.explanation: Traditional ML monitoring (accuracy, F1) is insufficient for LLMs because outputs are open-ended. LLM monitoring adds: (1) LLM-as-judge scoring for response quality, (2) hallucination detection (faithfulness to source), (3) safety filters for toxic/harmful content, (4) token cost tracking, (5) retrieval precision/recall for RAG. Tools: Langsmith, Arize AI, Weights & Biases Prompts, Trulens.
content.spanish: Los sistemas LLM en producción requieren monitoreo de la calidad de salida (fidelidad, relevancia, toxicidad), latencia y uso de tokens, y calidad de recuperación (para sistemas RAG). Las métricas tradicionales de ML son insuficientes para LLMs.
content.usage:
• Monitoring dashboard for production LLM: Faithfulness score (avg 0.87), Relevance score (avg 0.91), Toxicity rate (0.003%), p95 latency (1.2s), avg tokens/request (842), monthly token cost ($2,840).
  • Alert: "Faithfulness score dropped below 0.75 — likely retrieval degradation. Check vector DB freshness."
